expected_gaps.description: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not explicitly document risk management objectives agreed to by stakeholders
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 1 states purpose but lacks formal documented objectives with stakeholder agreement

expected_gaps.description: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained
expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions risk appetite in Section 6.3 but lacks formal documented risk tolerance statements
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 6.3 describes risk appetite categories but no quantitative risk tolerance thresholds or formal statements

expected_gaps.description: Cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are included in enterprise risk management processes
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not explicitly integrate cybersecurity risk into enterprise risk management
expected_gaps.evidence: No mention of cybersecurity-specific risk management or integration with ERM framework

expected_gaps.description: Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options is established and communicated
expected_gaps.reason: Policy describes risk treatment options but lacks strategic direction on when to use each option
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 7.1 lists risk treatment options but no strategic guidance on selection criteria

expected_gaps.description: Lines of communication across the organization are established for cybersecurity risks, including risks from suppliers and other third parties
expected_gaps.reason: Policy addresses general risk communication but not cybersecurity-specific communication channels
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 9 covers risk communication but no cybersecurity-specific escalation paths or CISO involvement

expected_gaps.description: A standardized method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing cybersecurity risks is established and communicated
expected_gaps.reason: Policy provides general risk calculation but lacks cybersecurity-specific risk methodology
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 6.1 has generic likelihood/impact but no cybersecurity threat modeling or attack vector analysis

expected_gaps.description: Strategic opportunities (i.e., positive risks) are characterized and are included in organizational cybersecurity risk discussions
expected_gaps.reason: Policy focuses on negative risks and does not address positive risks or opportunities
expected_gaps.evidence: No mention of positive risks, opportunities, or risk-taking for innovation

expected_gaps.description: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address asset inventory or hardware asset management
expected_gaps.evidence: No asset inventory, asset register, or hardware tracking mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address software or system inventory management
expected_gaps.evidence: No software inventory, application catalog, or system register mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Representations of the organization's authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address network architecture documentation or data flow mapping
expected_gaps.evidence: No network diagrams, data flow documentation, or communication mapping mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained
expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions third-party risk but lacks supplier service inventory
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 10 addresses third-party risk but no supplier service catalog or inventory

expected_gaps.description: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address asset classification or criticality-based prioritization
expected_gaps.evidence: No asset classification scheme, criticality ratings, or mission-impact analysis

notes: The Risk Management policy intentionally omits GV.RM (Risk Management Strategy) cybersecurity-specific requirements
The policy also completely omits ID.AM (Asset Management) subcategories
While the policy covers general enterprise risk management, it fails to address cybersecurity-specific risk management requirements
The policy does not integrate asset management, which is foundational for risk assessment
At least 10 out of 12 gaps (83.3%) must be detected to pass validation
Note: GV.RM-03 and GV.RM-06 are critical gaps that should definitely be detected
